head: post-A4 (5 fix agents + coordinator fix-up)
lens: Every contract (type interface, abstract method, declared invariant, typed-error class) is honored at EVERY call site. Probes: caller-side compliance for new Buffer side-channel, ImportConflictError reachability, LedgerCycleDetectedError handling, CommandQueuePersistenceLost handling, MCP/CLI error-translation completeness, staging-area lifecycle, family-of-sibling-call-site sweep on every Wave A4 named site.

findings.title: All 5 shipped examples violate KERNEL-B-007 contract — pass Buffer but no contentHash → throw ContentHashMismatchError at propose
findings.contract: proposeMutation({verb: 'ingest_artifact', payload: {filename, content: Buffer, mimeType, contentHash}}) — contentHash REQUIRED when content is Buffer (cluster-kernel.ts:506-528, throws ContentHashMismatchError on mismatch where claimedHash='' counts as '<missing>')
findings.violation: Every caller passes `content: Buffer` but omits `contentHash`. cluster-kernel.ts:512-516 reads `claimedHash = typeof payload.contentHash === 'string' ? payload.contentHash : ''`, computes `actualHash = sha256(buffer)`, then throws `ContentHashMismatchError('<missing>', actualHash)` because '' !== actualHash. The package ships `examples/` in package.json `files`, so users who follow the shipped examples hit this immediately. The five files are the canonical onboarding surface (research, agent-safe app, project-memory, generic, retrieval-bundle).
findings.recommendation: For each example, recompute the hash inline before propose: `const buf = Buffer.from(content); const hash = createHash('sha256').update(buf).digest('hex'); const cmd = await sdk.proposeMutation({ verb: 'ingest_artifact', targetStore: 'artifact', payload: { filename, content: buf, mimeType, contentHash: hash }, proposedBy: 'example' });` — exactly the shape used by test/phase15-proof.test.ts:200-213, test/wave6-proof.test.ts:48-60, test/wave-a3-tests-regression.test.ts:466-475, test/wave5-parity.test.ts:422-433. Either fix each example or expose a `kernel.ingestArtifactCommand(input)` helper that handles the hashing for callers.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep across 7 propose-site files yielded 5 NON-COMPLIANT (all in examples/), 7 COMPLIANT (test/phase15-proof.test.ts, test/wave6-proof.test.ts x2, test/wave-a3-tests-regression.test.ts, test/wave5-parity.test.ts, plus 1 intentional-violation `payload: {}` in test/policy-kernel.test.ts:557 which is a negative test). src/integrations/repo-knowledge/ingest.ts:244 uses kernel.ingestArtifact() helper (not the lifecycle path) — not affected. dashboard/* and src/dashboard/* contain only static demo data, no live propose calls.

findings.title: ImportConflictError contract is UNREACHABLE via the documented restore() consumer — STORES-B-003 hole persists end-to-end
findings.contract: ImportConflictError (src/adapters/local/errors.ts:71-97) — 'thrown by import* methods … when an existing record with the same id is found but its content differs … Pre-fix the existing record was silently returned, masking tampered backups whose ids collided with live ledger entries'
findings.violation: Wave A4 added the content-conflict check INSIDE the four import* methods. But the ONLY documented consumer (`ops/backup.ts::restore`) short-circuits with `exists(id)` BEFORE calling import*. A tampered backup whose entity/artifact/event/receipt id matches a live record is still silently masked — restore reports `skipped++`, the operator sees a clean RestoreResult, the tampered intent never reaches the typed-error path. The unit-level fix (verified by test/wave-a4-stores-regression.test.ts:375-446) does not propagate to the integration path. STORES-B-003's claim 'restore reported skipped but the cluster ended up with the original record while the tampered intent went undetected' (errors.ts:65-66) describes EXACTLY the current restore() behavior.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) remove the `exists()` short-circuit in restore() and always call `importSnapshot/importEvent/importReceipt`, letting the new content-compare surface conflicts (idempotent on byte-identical, ImportConflictError on mismatch); or (b) on `exists=true`, fetch the existing record and call `assertContentMatch` from backup.ts before incrementing `skipped`. Add an integration test: `restore(tampered-backup-with-same-id)` should populate `result.entities.errors[*]` with the ImportConflictError message. The current Wave A4 unit tests verify the contract at the store level but no test exercises the restore() consumer path.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep across all four restore arms (entities L117-129, artifacts L142-168, events L179-191, receipts L199-211): ALL four short-circuit identically. No siblings comply; all four siblings need the same fix.

findings.title: Adapter errors (ImportConflictError, LedgerCycleDetectedError, CorruptStoreError, InvalidContentHashError) extend plain Error — collapse to INTERNAL_ERROR via redactError, losing the stable code SURFACE-B-003 promised
findings.contract: redactError (src/mcp/sanitize.ts:170-199) — 'For typed ClusterError subclasses (NotFoundError, CommandRejectedError, ContentHashMismatchError, …) err.code is used. For built-in JS errors … a stable INTERNAL_<KIND>_ERROR code is mapped. Anything else collapses to INTERNAL_ERROR.'
findings.violation: redactError's typed-error branch only catches `ClusterError` subclasses. The four adapter-side typed errors all extend plain `Error`, so they fall through to the second branch where `BUILTIN_ERROR_CODES[err.constructor.name]` returns undefined (the map only has TypeError/RangeError/SyntaxError/ReferenceError/URIError/EvalError) → defaults to `INTERNAL_ERROR`. The MCP host sees `code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR'` for ALL adapter failures — cannot distinguish corrupt-store from cycle-detection from content-conflict from invalid-hash. The whole point of the new STORES-B-003 and STORES-B-015 typed errors was to give consumers a stable signal; that signal dies at the MCP boundary.
findings.recommendation: Two options. (a) Promote each adapter error to `ClusterError` and assign a stable code: `CorruptStoreError → STORE_CORRUPT`, `InvalidContentHashError → INVALID_CONTENT_HASH`, `ImportConflictError → IMPORT_CONFLICT`, `LedgerCycleDetectedError → LEDGER_CYCLE_DETECTED`. This is intrusive (adapters/ would need to import from kernel/, breaking the no-back-edge rule) so the comment at kernel/errors.ts:67-73 explicitly forbids it. (b) Add an explicit `ADAPTER_ERROR_CODES` map to sanitize.ts keyed on `err.constructor.name` and consult it before falling to the default. (b) is the minimal-impact fix and preserves the kernel-vs-adapter import boundary.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep across all four adapter errors: ALL FOUR extend plain Error and ALL FOUR collapse to INTERNAL_ERROR. ImportSnapshotNotSupportedError (ops/errors.ts:15) also extends plain Error — same problem. Six adapter/ops typed-errors, zero stable MCP codes. ClusterError descendants (NotFoundError, ContentHashMismatchError, StagedContentTamperedError, CommandQueuePersistenceLostError, etc.) all comply.

findings.title: backup() does NOT include `.db-cluster/pending-content/` — restored cluster with proposed ingest_artifact commands throws StagedContentTamperedError on commit
findings.contract: Backup & restore preserves identity, provenance, mutation history, and policy state (ops/backup.ts:1-3 module header). Wave A4 staging-area contract (cluster-kernel.ts:483-505): 'proposeMutation stages Buffer to .db-cluster/pending-content/{contentHash}; commitMutation reads it back and re-validates the hash; StagedContentTamperedError('<missing>') if the staging file is absent at commit'.
findings.violation: Wave A4 introduced a per-command staging file outside the four truth stores. Proposed-but-uncommitted ingest_artifact commands carry only the contentHash in `payload.content`; the actual bytes live in `.db-cluster/pending-content/{contentHash}`. backup() captures `commands` (option.commandQueue.list()) but NEVER captures the staging directory. Restore on a fresh dataDir → the staging dir is empty → `commitMutation` for any restored proposed-ingest command throws `StagedContentTamperedError(contentHash, stagingPath, '<missing>')`. The backup/restore contract claim 'preserves mutation history' is violated for the proposed-state subset of mutations.
findings.recommendation: Add `stagedContent?: Record<contentHash, base64>` to `ClusterBackup`. In backup(), enumerate pending-content/ files (each named by sha256 hex), base64-encode their bytes, and stamp them in. In restore(), if `data.stagedContent` is present and commands include a proposed ingest_artifact, write each staged buffer to `<dataDir>/pending-content/{contentHash}` (after sha256 verification mirroring the artifactSnapshots path L150-154). Alternatively, document that proposed ingest_artifact commands MUST be either committed or rejected before backup — but that's a policy limitation, not a contract guarantee.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep across all ops/ surfaces: backup.ts (no pending-content handling), doctor.ts (no orphan-staging check — see V1-A4-005), verify.ts (no staging verification), rebuild.ts (no staging awareness), migrations.ts (no staging migration). All five ops surfaces are unaware of the new staging area.

findings.title: doctor() and verify() have NO check for orphaned pending-content/ staging files — promised observability surface for the new staging area is missing
findings.contract: cluster-kernel.ts:482-505 staging-area docstring promises: 'crash mid-write leaves the prior good state'. The implicit operator-facing contract (mirroring mutation_orphaned for the orphan signal): orphan staging files from rejected/abandoned proposes should be visible via ops/health surfaces. doctor.ts:209-249 explicitly mirrors verify.ts's mutation_orphaned check 'so both ops surfaces see the orphan signal' (V1-007 fix-up).
findings.violation: The staging-area contract (cluster-kernel.ts:131-142) says rejected/compensated commands have their staging files cleaned 'best-effort, silent on error … the next sweep can clear'. No sweep exists. doctor() does not query for orphans (e.g. staging files whose contentHash is referenced by no command in the queue and no committed artifact in artifact-store). A long-running cluster with rejected ingest_artifact proposes accumulates pending-content files indefinitely. The V1-007 precedent (mutation_orphaned consumer parity between doctor and verify) demands the same pattern here.
findings.recommendation: Add a `no_orphan_staging` check to doctor.ts: enumerate `<dataDir>/pending-content/*`, intersect against commandQueue's set of proposed ingest_artifact contentHashes + artifact-store's set of contentHashes. Report any staging file that matches neither as degraded with severity: warning. Mirror in verify.ts. Add an `db-cluster ops sweep-staging` CLI subcommand for forced cleanup. This closes the orphan-staging blind spot before it becomes a disk-bloat incident.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep: doctor.ts has the no_orphaned_mutations check (Wave A3 V1-007 fix-up) — the only ops orphan signal it consumes. The staging-orphan signal is the obvious second member of the family; not added. verify.ts mirrors doctor for mutation_orphaned (verify.ts:154-189) but does not mirror it for staging.

findings.title: cluster_trace MCP arm spreads ProvenanceGraph nodes raw — node.label / node.metadata leak entity names, actorIds, resultSummary text (sibling of SURFACE-B-001 LIST-leak, missed)
findings.contract: SURFACE-B-001 fix doctrine (Wave A4): list-shaped MCP arms must apply per-record sanitization (cluster_find_sources fix). MCP-output baseline (sanitize.ts:11-15): 'even with an empty policy set, no MCP tool ever emits storagePath' — generalizes to 'no MCP tool emits raw entity/event/receipt text' for the same threat model.
findings.violation: Wave A4's SURFACE-B-001 fix closed the same shape on cluster_find_sources (L480-484 now wraps each record with sanitizeIndexRecordForOutput). cluster_trace returns a structurally similar nested-record list — graph.nodes[*] — and was missed. Each node label exposes entity-name (`'concept: federated-truth'`), event actorId (`'entity_created by alice@example.com'`), and receipt resultSummary text verbatim. The no-policy path (~614 baseline tests) returns graph.nodes raw across the MCP boundary. This is the EXACT sibling-LIST-leak pattern Wave A4 named in the surface-findings report.
findings.recommendation: Add a `sanitizeProvenanceNodeForOutput` helper to policy/store-output-sanitizers.ts that strips `metadata` to a whitelist (timestamp + uri + ownerStore + isSourceTruth) and replaces `label` with a `_labelPolicy: 'opaque'` marker (or a redacted summary). Apply it in cluster_trace: `nodes: graph.nodes.map(sanitizeProvenanceNodeForOutput), edges: graph.edges, summary: graph.summary, ...`. Same shape as SURFACE-B-001's cluster_find_sources fix. Family-check: cluster_trace, cluster_resolve (already covered per V1-002 Wave A3), cluster_list_receipts (receipts already sanitized), cluster_retrieve_bundle (artifacts/entities already sanitized but provenanceEventIds are returned raw).
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep across MCP list-shaped returns: cluster_find_sources (FIXED Wave A4), cluster_resolve (FIXED Wave A3 V1-002), cluster_retrieve_bundle (entities/artifacts wrapped, provenance event ids returned only as ids — safer), cluster_trace (RAW — this finding), cluster_list_receipts (receipts wrapped). cluster_trace is the one outstanding member of the family.

findings.title: compareRetrieval propagates LedgerCycleDetectedError raw across the integration boundary — STORES-B-015 contract says trace consumers must handle it
findings.contract: LedgerCycleDetectedError (adapters/local/errors.ts:138-152) — 'thrown by LocalLedgerStore.trace() … surfaces the corruption loudly with the visited path so the operator can locate and excise the cycle'. The implicit caller contract: typed-error must be either translated or surfaced as actionable to the caller. Wave A4's STORES-B-015 fix lands the throw but does not migrate any caller.
findings.violation: STORES-B-015's fix-or-loud principle (errors.ts:128-136: 'append-only stores can still be tampered with on-disk; trace must not trust the parent chain blindly') gives consumers a typed error. compareRetrieval does NOT translate it — a single tampered ledger event mid-iteration kills the entire comparison report, the operator sees a raw stack trace, no per-query partial results. The whole point of STORES-B-015 is to surface the corruption AND let the consumer continue with degraded results.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the trace call: `try { events = await kernel.traceProvenance(resolved.object.id); } catch (err) { if (err instanceof LedgerCycleDetectedError) { result.ledgerCycleDetected = true; result.ledgerCycleEventIds = err.eventIds; provenanceEvents += 0; continue; } throw err; }`. Add a `ledgerCycleDetected?: boolean` field to ComparisonResult shape. The MCP equivalent (cluster_trace) already has TraceBuilder's URI-level visited set so the typed error doesn't surface there; the SDK flat-event path (compareRetrieval, anyone calling kernel.traceProvenance directly) is the unprotected surface.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep across kernel.traceProvenance callers: PolicyEnforcedKernel.traceProvenance (policy-enforced-kernel.ts:796-804) — propagates raw, no catch. compareRetrieval (above) — propagates raw, no catch. ZERO consumers handle the new typed error. The MCP surface (cluster_trace) uses traceObject which has its own URI-level visited set (trace-builder.ts:75) — the ledger.trace cycle path is not exercised from the MCP boundary, so the typed-error surfaces nowhere visible to operators today.

findings.title: CommandQueuePersistenceLostError throws from lazy load() — getKernel() does NOT pre-check; first kernel op after a marker-but-no-file state surfaces raw
findings.contract: CommandQueuePersistenceLostError (kernel/errors.ts:107-122) — 'distinct from CommandQueueCorruptError because the cause is persistence loss, not corruption … Recovery: restore from a backup that includes both files, or delete the marker file to re-cold-start'. The implicit operator-facing contract: surface this as a startup-time signal (mirroring CorruptStoreError) so operators don't lose data through silent fallthrough.
findings.violation: The marker-file mechanism (TESTS-B-003) is designed to make persistence-loss loud, BUT the load is lazy. A user who runs `db-cluster doctor` first (which doesn't touch CommandQueue) sees no signal. Then they run `db-cluster commit <id>` and see the typed error — but at that point they have already issued doctor and possibly believed the cluster is healthy. The contract says 'fail loudly so the operator sees a typed signal' — that's lazy, so the operator sees it AFTER the first command-write attempt. doctor() should pre-check the marker-vs-file state and report it as a degraded check.
findings.recommendation: Add to doctor.ts: a `command_queue_persistence_consistent` check that calls `existsSync(<dataDir>/pending-commands.json)` and `existsSync(<dataDir>/command-queue-marker)`, reports degraded+warning when marker exists but file is absent (mirrors the load() short-circuit at command-queue.ts:83-85), suggested command: 'restore from backup or delete the marker'. doctor must not THROW — it should report. The current lazy throw is fine as a hard signal at command-write time; doctor adds proactive surfacing.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep: CorruptStoreError (adapters/local/errors.ts:20) is also lazy — only thrown at first load(), which happens in adapter constructors. Adapter constructors are eager (`this.entities = this.load()` at local-canonical-store.ts:30, similar in local-artifact-store.ts:51, local-ledger-store.ts:36-37, local-index-store.ts:27). So adapter corruption IS eager. CommandQueue is the inconsistent sibling — constructor doesn't pre-load. Fixing parity: either move `this.load()` to the constructor (eager, lossless), or add the doctor.ts check (lazy at first op, eager via diagnostic).

findings.title: CLI `trace <uri>`, `lineage <uri>`, `trace-bundle <query>`, `db-cluster trace --graph` lack try/catch — LedgerCycleDetectedError and CommandQueuePersistenceLostError print raw stack traces
findings.contract: Wave A4 SURFACE-B-004 acknowledgement (in stage-b-audit/agent-surface-findings.md) — 'Of ~30 CLI subcommands, only ~10 wrap the kernel call in try/catch … Result: raw stack traces in operator-facing terminal output'. The fix was NOT applied in Wave A4; only the policy explain/test arms were fixed. trace/lineage/trace-bundle remain bare.
findings.violation: trace/lineage/trace-bundle call into kernel.traceObject (URI-level visited set, no LedgerCycleDetectedError) — but they ALSO trigger CommandQueue lazy load via kernel constructors (the first time getKernel() initialized today is via init/ingest sequence). If the marker-vs-file inconsistency is present from a prior crashed propose, the user running `db-cluster trace <uri>` sees a raw Node stack trace. The same applies if the ledger has a cycle — cluster_trace through MCP is silently visited-set-protected, but a CLI `trace <uri>` that calls traceObject is the same (no cycle exposure). The HIGH-severity gap is the cycle path's non-translation; the MEDIUM is the bare-stack UX everywhere else.
findings.recommendation: Apply the SURFACE-B-004 wrapper pattern: `async function safeAction(name, fn) { try { await fn(); } catch (err) { console.error(`${name} failed: ${err.message}`); if (err instanceof CommandQueuePersistenceLostError) console.error(`Recovery: ${err.filePath} / ${err.markerPath}`); if (err instanceof LedgerCycleDetectedError) console.error(`Cycle event ids: ${err.eventIds.join(' → ')}`); process.exit(1); } }`. Wrap every .action(async ...) body. The lifecycle commands at cli.ts:391-394 demonstrate the correct shape.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep of CLI subcommands: ~20 unwrapped (ingest, entity create, link, find, propose, receipts, index rebuild/status/explain/stale, retrieve, explain-retrieval, trace, why, lineage, trace-bundle, doctor, verify, rebuild, backup), ~10 wrapped (commit, approve, reject, compensate, inspect-command, validate, plus 4 lifecycle siblings). The stage-b-audit/agent-surface-findings.md SURFACE-B-004 already enumerated the gap; Wave A4 closed policy explain/test and the release-gate stdout-slice issues but the wide CLI safety wrapper migration is unstarted.

findings.title: ContentHashMismatchError / StagedContentTamperedError / BufferSideChannelNotSupportedError thrown from kernel — CLI ingest path has NO operator-friendly translation; raw .message printed
findings.contract: These three errors are ClusterError subclasses with stable codes (CONTENT_HASH_MISMATCH, STAGED_CONTENT_TAMPERED, BUFFER_SIDE_CHANNEL_NOT_SUPPORTED). Each carries diagnosable detail: ContentHashMismatchError.claimedHash + actualHash, StagedContentTamperedError.stagingPath. The MCP boundary maps them via redactError correctly. The CLI does not.
findings.violation: Operator running `db-cluster propose '{"verb":"ingest_artifact", "payload": {...}}'` with a bad hash sees `Command failed: Content hash mismatch on propose: caller claimed ... but sha256(content)=...` — that's the err.message, fine but raw. StagedContentTamperedError exposes stagingPath in its message — a filesystem path that the SURFACE-B-003 redactError helper would scrub at the MCP boundary, but the CLI prints it raw. The CLI is operator-facing so the path is fine for trusted-operator UX, but the typed-error structure (claimedHash, actualHash, stagingPath) is lost — operators get a long single-line message instead of a structured 'here's what to do' panel.
findings.recommendation: Add a `formatClusterError(err)` helper in cli.ts that switches on `err.code`: CONTENT_HASH_MISMATCH → 'Hash mismatch: caller claimed {claimedHash}, computed {actualHash}. Recompute sha256(content) and retry.' STAGED_CONTENT_TAMPERED → 'Tampered staging file at {stagingPath}. Inspect the file for forensics, then re-propose with fresh content.' BUFFER_SIDE_CHANNEL_NOT_SUPPORTED → '{adapterName} does not support buffer ingest; use a local adapter or migrate the dataDir.' Mirror SURFACE-B-003's structured-error shape on the CLI side. Bonus: prints scrubbed-path version of stagingPath when --safe-output is set, mirroring DEBUG=1 inverse.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep: every ClusterError subclass exposes typed fields (NotFoundError.id, CommandRejectedError.commandId+reason, ReceiptFailedError.subjectId+cause). The CLI prints `err.message` raw at every catch site (cli.ts:70, 146, 305, 391, 414, 442, 462, 483, 498, 573, 666, 961, 1003). Zero subclass-aware formatting. The MCP side is structured (redactError returns {code, message}); the CLI side is unstructured.

findings.title: In-memory kernel mode (no dataDir) bypasses staging-area contract — ingest_artifact payload retains raw Buffer; formatCommandOutput → JSON.stringify leaks bytes through inspectCommand surfaces
findings.contract: cluster-kernel.ts:97-102 'When omitted (in-memory kernel) Buffer payloads on ingest_artifact still flow through commitMutation via the in-memory command map without touching disk — the staging-area path is bypassed and content is held in the payload itself, which is safe because no JSON round-trip happens.' This safety claim breaks when the in-memory command IS subsequently JSON-stringified for output.
findings.violation: In production MCP path (server.ts:24, CLUSTER_DIR always present), this is unreachable — the staging area always exists. But the `sdkOverride` test path constructs ClusterSDK with no clusterDir, instantiates an in-memory ClusterKernel, and routes inspectCommand through formatCommandOutput. The artifact bytes appear as `{type:'Buffer', data:[...]}` in the JSON output. This isn't a production MCP leak; it's a test-path leak that obscures the safety claim 'no JSON round-trip happens' (cluster-kernel.ts:101). The claim is FALSE for inspect-command on in-memory mode.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) update the in-memory-mode comment at cluster-kernel.ts:99-102 to say 'Buffer payloads are kept in-memory until commit; surfacing them through inspectCommand requires opt-in (--unsafe-raw)'; or (b) compute contentHash even in in-memory mode and replace payload.content with the hash string (mirror the disk-mode rewrite at L523-527, just skip the staging write). (b) is the cleaner fix — payload shape becomes identical across both modes, formatCommandOutput surface stays safe by construction.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep across other Buffer payloads: only `ingest_artifact` ever carries Buffer payload. Other verbs (create_entity, update_entity, link_evidence, reindex, compensate) are JSON-clean. The in-memory-mode hole is ingest-specific. Could resurface if a future verb (e.g. attachment_uploaded, bulk_import) introduces Buffer payloads without going through the same staging gate.

findings.title: test/wave6-proof.test.ts:57 uses `mediaType: 'text/markdown'` but kernel destructures `mimeType` — silent typo; artifact lands with mimeType=undefined
findings.contract: kernel's ingest_artifact commit-arm destructures `{ filename, content, mimeType, contentHash }` from payload (cluster-kernel.ts:631-636). The kernel.ingestArtifact helper accepts IngestArtifactInput { filename, content, mimeType, actorId } (cluster-kernel.ts:41-46).
findings.violation: Test typo. The artifact gets ingested with `mimeType: undefined`. The test doesn't assert on mimeType so no failure. The downstream Artifact.mimeType is undefined-typed but the type system tolerates it via the `unknown` payload cast. This is a quality-of-test issue, not a contract violation.
findings.recommendation: Trivial: change `mediaType` to `mimeType` in test/wave6-proof.test.ts:57. Cosmetic.
findings.family_check: Sibling sweep: other Wave A4 ingest_artifact test sites all use `mimeType` correctly (phase15-proof.test.ts:209, wave-a3-tests-regression.test.ts:471, wave5-parity.test.ts:428). Just the one typo.

summary: Wave A4 closed 12 contract gaps inside the kernel + stores + surface domains and added 7 new typed errors. Three of those contracts are not honored end-to-end. (1) ImportConflictError is fully verified at unit-test level but UNREACHABLE through the documented restore() consumer because restore short-circuits on exists() before any import* method can fire — the STORES-B-003 silent-tampering hole persists for the only consumer the contract was supposed to protect. (2) Four adapter-tier typed errors (ImportConflictError, LedgerCycleDetectedError, CorruptStoreError, InvalidContentHashError, ImportSnapshotNotSupportedError) all extend plain Error rather than ClusterError, so MCP's redactError collapses them to INTERNAL_ERROR — the stable-code promise of SURFACE-B-003 doesn't reach the adapter layer. (3) Wave A4's new staging-area at .db-cluster/pending-content/ is invisible to backup/restore/doctor/verify — a backup containing proposed ingest_artifact commands cannot be restored coherently, and orphan staging files have no health-check consumer. The sibling-LIST-leak pattern that SURFACE-B-001 fixed for cluster_find_sources remains live on cluster_trace. Five shipped examples still use the pre-Wave-A4 propose signature and will throw ContentHashMismatchError on the first run. The new typed errors are well-defined at their throw site, but the wave didn't migrate enough call sites to honor them end-to-end.
lens_quality_self_assessment: V1 is contract-completeness: typed abstraction promised, every caller honors it. Overlap with V2 (cross-boundary) is unavoidable on V1-A4-006 (cluster_trace LIST-leak — V2 will also see this as a boundary issue; V1 frames it as a sibling-of-Wave-A4-fix contract violation, V2 as a kernel↔MCP boundary leak — both lenses surface the same site but with different framing). Overlap with V3 (invariant/test-completeness) is highest on V1-A4-002 (restore short-circuit) — V3 may flag the absence of an integration test exercising restore+tampered-backup; V1 flags the contract-unreachability that the missing test would have caught. The cleanest V1-only finding is V1-A4-001 (examples) because the examples are not subject to V2 boundary analysis and may not be exercised by any test (V3). The biggest V1 blind spot: I cannot probe runtime behavior — every finding rests on static-source analysis of contract docstrings, type signatures, and grep-found call sites. A future migration that introduces a new ingest_artifact caller path (e.g. a streaming uploader) would need a re-run of the family-of-call-sites probe; today the answer is 'no surface known' but tomorrow it could regress silently. V1 also under-weights documentation drift — the handbook.md does not document the contentHash requirement (V1-A4-001 sibling concern), and that gap is not severity-CRITICAL the way runtime-throws are.
